9	"""TrainerCore + TrainerController: the trainer-boundary split.
10	
11	``TrainerCore`` runs exactly one branch-free step (strategy forward/loss, backend
12	backward/step) plus the grad-accumulation boundary. ``TrainerController`` owns
13	the lifecycle: fit / evaluate / save_checkpoint. EAGLE3 and DFlash share this
14	unchanged — only the strategy differs.
15	"""
16	
17	from __future__ import annotations
18	
19	import itertools
20	import logging
21	import os
22	import sys
23	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
24	from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional
25	
26	import torch
27	
28	from specforge.runtime.contracts import TrainBatch
29	from specforge.training.backend import TrainingBackend
30	from specforge.training.strategies.base import (
31	    DraftTrainStrategy,
32	    StepContext,
33	    StepOutput,
34	)
35	
36	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
37	
38	
39	@dataclass(frozen=True)
40	class Checkpoint:
41	    """A saved training checkpoint location (resume target) — deliberately NOT a
42	    published weight version (weight publication is not implemented)."""
43	
44	    checkpoint_uri: str
45	    global_step: int
46	    epoch: int
47	    strategy: str
48	    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
49	
50	
51	@dataclass(frozen=True)
52	class StepResult:
53	    """Result of one TrainerCore step; ``optimizer_stepped`` is the authoritative
54	    grad-accumulation boundary signal."""
55	
56	    optimizer_stepped: bool
57	    loss: float
58	    grad_norm: Optional[float]
59	    metrics: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
60	
61	
62	def _scalar(x: Any) -> float:
63	    if isinstance(x, torch.Tensor):
64	        return float(x.detach().float().mean().item())
65	    if isinstance(x, (list, tuple)) and x:
66	        return float(torch.stack([t.detach().float() for t in x]).mean().item())
67	    return float(x)
68	
69	
70	def _dp_mean_scalars(
71	    values: Dict[str, torch.Tensor], *, process_group: Any = None
72	) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
73	    """Average scalar metrics across DP ranks with one collective.
74	
75	    Uses the established DFlash metric convention (DP mean):
76	    without it the disagg consumer logs a single rank's local-batch accuracy
77	    (~1 rank x batch x anchors), which is ~sqrt(world) noisier and spikes because
78	    each rank's few round-robin refs can be all-easy or all-hard. Reducing across
79	    ranks recovers the ~world x larger effective sample the stock path logs.
80	    The caller invokes this only at an optimizer boundary: intermediate
81	    micro-step metrics are not logged, so synchronizing them only adds latency.
82	    """
83	    import torch.distributed as dist
84	
85	    if not values or not (dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized()):
86	        return values
87	    world = (
88	        dist.get_world_size()
89	        if process_group is None
90	        else dist.get_world_size(group=process_group)
91	    )
92	    if world <= 1:
93	        return values
94	    names = list(values)
95	    packed = torch.stack([values[name].detach().float().reshape(()) for name in names])
96	    if process_group is None:
97	        dist.all_reduce(packed)
98	    else:
99	        dist.all_reduce(packed, group=process_group)
100	    packed /= world
101	    return {name: packed[index] for index, name in enumerate(names)}
102	
103	
104	def _reduce_ratio_metrics(
105	    values: Dict[str, Any],
106	    *,
107	    device: torch.device,
108	    process_group: Any,
109	    reduce: bool,
110	) -> Dict[str, float]:
111	    """Form telemetry ratios only after summing their numerators and counts."""
112	
113	    if not values:
114	        return {}
115	    normalized = []
116	    for name in sorted(values):
117	        pair = values[name]
118	        if not isinstance(pair, (tuple, list)) or len(pair) != 2:
119	            raise TypeError(
120	                f"ratio metric {name!r} must be a (numerator, denominator) pair"
121	            )
122	        numerator = torch.as_tensor(pair[0]).detach().float().flatten().to(device)
123	        denominator = torch.as_tensor(pair[1]).detach().float().flatten().to(device)
124	        if numerator.shape != denominator.shape:
125	            raise ValueError(
126	                f"ratio metric {name!r} shape mismatch: "
127	                f"{tuple(numerator.shape)} vs {tuple(denominator.shape)}"
128	            )
129	        normalized.append((name, numerator, denominator))
130	
131	    packed = torch.cat(
132	        [
133	            tensor
134	            for _, numerator, denominator in normalized
135	            for tensor in (numerator, denominator)
136	        ]
137	    )
138	    if reduce:
139	        import torch.distributed as dist
140	
141	        if dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized():
142	            if process_group is None:
143	                dist.all_reduce(packed)
144	            else:
145	                dist.all_reduce(packed, group=process_group)
146	
147	    output: Dict[str, float] = {}
148	    cursor = 0
149	    for name, numerator, _denominator in normalized:
150	        width = numerator.numel()
151	        summed_numerator = packed[cursor : cursor + width]
152	        cursor += width
153	        summed_denominator = packed[cursor : cursor + width]
154	        cursor += width
155	        ratios = summed_numerator / summed_denominator.clamp_min(1e-12)
156	        if width == 1:
157	            output[name] = float(ratios.item())
158	        else:
159	            output.update(
160	                {f"{name}_{index}": float(value) for index, value in enumerate(ratios)}
161	            )
162	    return output
163	
164	
165	_EAGLE3_STRUCTURED_METRIC_KEYS = frozenset(
166	    {
167	        "acces",
168	        "acceptance_rates",
169	        "plosses",
170	        "acc_corrects",
171	        "acc_denoms",
172	        "metric_losses",
173	        "metric_loss_denoms",
174	    }
175	)
176	
177	
178	def _metric_vector(values: Any, *, device: torch.device, name: str) -> torch.Tensor:
179	    """Normalize one per-TTT metric sequence without losing its positions."""
180	    if isinstance(values, torch.Tensor):
181	        vector = values.detach().flatten()
182	    elif isinstance(values, (list, tuple)):
183	        vector = torch.stack(
184	            [torch.as_tensor(value).detach().reshape(()) for value in values]
185	        )
186	    else:
187	        raise TypeError(f"{name} must be a tensor or sequence, got {type(values)!r}")
188	    if vector.numel() == 0:
189	        raise ValueError(f"{name} must contain at least one TTT position")
190	    return vector.to(device=device, dtype=torch.float32)
191	
192	
193	def _reduce_eagle3_metrics(
194	    raw: Dict[str, Any],
195	    *,
196	    device: torch.device,
197	    process_group: Any,
198	    ploss_decay: float,
199	) -> Optional[Dict[str, float]]:
200	    """Reduce EAGLE3's per-position training telemetry as numerators/counts.
201	
202	    Accuracy and p-loss are ratios, so averaging rank-local ratios biases the
203	    result whenever ranks carry different token counts.  Pack every numerator
204	    and denominator into one collective and form ratios only after the global
205	    SUM.  Acceptance rate has no separate count in the model contract; weight
206	    it by the corresponding p-loss token count, matching the evaluator's
207	    batch-size-invariant convention.
208	    """
209	    required = {
210	        "acc_corrects",
211	        "acc_denoms",
212	        "metric_losses",
213	        "metric_loss_denoms",
214	    }
215	    if not required.issubset(raw):
216	        return None
217	
218	    corrects = _metric_vector(raw["acc_corrects"], device=device, name="acc_corrects")
219	    acc_denoms = _metric_vector(raw["acc_denoms"], device=device, name="acc_denoms")
220	    losses = _metric_vector(raw["metric_losses"], device=device, name="metric_losses")
221	    loss_denoms = _metric_vector(
222	        raw["metric_loss_denoms"], device=device, name="metric_loss_denoms"
223	    )
224	    length = corrects.numel()
225	    vectors = {
226	        "acc_denoms": acc_denoms,
227	        "metric_losses": losses,
228	        "metric_loss_denoms": loss_denoms,
229	    }
230	    acceptance_rates = None
231	    if "acceptance_rates" in raw:
232	        acceptance_rates = _metric_vector(
233	            raw["acceptance_rates"], device=device, name="acceptance_rates"
234	        )
235	        vectors["acceptance_rates"] = acceptance_rates
236	    mismatched = {
237	        name: value.numel()
238	        for name, value in vectors.items()
239	        if value.numel() != length
240	    }
241	    if mismatched:
242	        raise ValueError(
243	            "EAGLE3 structured metric lengths must match acc_corrects "
244	            f"({length}); got {mismatched}"
245	        )
246	
247	    # Rows: accuracy numerator/denominator, p-loss numerator/denominator,
248	    # acceptance numerator/denominator.  The last two rows stay zero when the
249	    # strategy does not expose acceptance telemetry.
250	    packed = torch.stack(
251	        (
252	            corrects,
253	            acc_denoms,
254	            losses * loss_denoms,
255	            loss_denoms,
256	            (
257	                acceptance_rates * loss_denoms
258	                if acceptance_rates is not None
259	                else torch.zeros_like(loss_denoms)
260	            ),
261	            (
262	                loss_denoms
263	                if acceptance_rates is not None
264	                else torch.zeros_like(loss_denoms)
265	            ),
266	        )
267	    )
268	
269	    import torch.distributed as dist
270	
271	    if dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized():
272	        world = dist.get_world_size(process_group)
273	        if world > 1:
274	            dist.all_reduce(packed, op=dist.ReduceOp.SUM, group=process_group)
275	
276	    reduced_acc = packed[0] / packed[1].clamp_min(1e-6)
277	    reduced_ploss = packed[2] / packed[3].clamp_min(1e-6)
278	    result: Dict[str, float] = {}
279	    for index, value in enumerate(reduced_acc.tolist()):
280	        result[f"acc_{index}"] = float(value)
281	    for index, value in enumerate(reduced_ploss.tolist()):
282	        result[f"ploss_{index}"] = float(value)
283	
284	    result["acc"] = float(packed[0].sum().div(packed[1].sum().clamp_min(1e-6)).item())
285	    weights = torch.tensor(
286	        [ploss_decay**index for index in range(length)],
287	        dtype=reduced_ploss.dtype,
288	        device=reduced_ploss.device,
289	    )
290	    result["loss"] = float((reduced_ploss * weights).sum().item())
291	
292	    if acceptance_rates is not None:
293	        reduced_acceptance = packed[4] / packed[5].clamp_min(1e-6)
294	        for index, value in enumerate(reduced_acceptance.tolist()):
295	            result[f"acceptance_rate_{index}"] = float(value)
296	        result["acceptance_rate"] = float(reduced_acceptance.mean().item())
297	    return result
298	
299	
300	class TrainerCore:
301	    """One step: forward/loss (strategy) -> backward (backend) -> optimizer boundary."""
302	
303	    def __init__(
304	        self,
305	        strategy: DraftTrainStrategy,
306	        backend: TrainingBackend,
307	        *,
308	        accumulation_steps: int = 1,
309	    ) -> None:
310	        self.strategy = strategy
311	        self.backend = backend
312	        self.accumulation_steps = max(1, accumulation_steps)
313	        self._micro = 0
314	
315	    @property
316	    def accumulation_remainder(self) -> int:
317	        """Micro-batches whose gradients have not reached an optimizer step."""
318	        return self._micro % self.accumulation_steps
319	
320	    def train_step(
321	        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
322	    ) -> StepResult:
323	        out: StepOutput = self.strategy.forward_loss(batch, ctx)
324	        loss = out.loss / self.accumulation_steps
325	        self._micro += 1
326	        # The boundary is known before backward so the backend can defer the FSDP
327	        # gradient reduction (no_sync) on non-boundary micro-steps.
328	        stepped = self._micro % self.accumulation_steps == 0
329	        self.backend.backward(loss, is_boundary=stepped)
330	        grad_norm = self.backend.step() if stepped else None
331	        return self._result(out, grad_norm, stepped)
332	
333	    def _result(self, out: StepOutput, grad_norm, stepped: bool) -> StepResult:
334	        # EAGLE3 carries per-TTT numerators and denominators.  Preserve those
335	        # positions and reduce counts before ratios; scalarizing its lists here
336	        # would both collapse the TTT structure and log one rank's local data.
337	        metric_device = (
338	            out.loss.device
339	            if isinstance(out.loss, torch.Tensor)
340	            else torch.device("cpu")
341	        )
342	        parallel_config = getattr(self.backend, "parallel_config", None)
343	        process_group = getattr(parallel_config, "fsdp_process_group", None)
344	        structured = _reduce_eagle3_metrics(
345	            out.metrics,
346	            device=metric_device,
347	            process_group=process_group,
348	            ploss_decay=float(getattr(self.strategy, "ploss_decay", 1.0)),
349	        )
350	        # Structured EAGLE3 metrics are already globally reduced.  Remaining
351	        # scalar diagnostics are DP-averaged in a single collective at optimizer
352	        # boundaries; non-boundary results stay rank-local.
353	        metrics: Dict[str, Any] = dict(structured or {})
354	        metrics.update(
355	            _reduce_ratio_metrics(
356	                out.ratio_metrics,
357	                device=metric_device,
358	                process_group=process_group,
359	                reduce=stepped,
360	            )
361	        )
362	        scalar_metrics: Dict[str, torch.Tensor] = {}
363	        if "loss" not in metrics:
364	            scalar_metrics["loss"] = out.loss
365	        if "accuracy" in out.metrics and "acc" not in metrics:
366	            accuracy = out.metrics["accuracy"]
367	            if isinstance(accuracy, torch.Tensor):
368	                scalar_metrics["acc"] = (
369	                    accuracy.detach().float().mean().to(out.loss.device)
370	                )
371	            elif isinstance(accuracy, (int, float)) and not isinstance(accuracy, bool):
372	                scalar_metrics["acc"] = out.loss.detach().new_tensor(float(accuracy))
373	        # Strategies may expose additional scalar diagnostics without teaching
374	        # the generic trainer their algorithm-specific names. Move CPU schedule
375	        # scalars (for example Domino's lambda_base) onto the loss device before
376	        # the DP reduction so NCCL-backed runs do not all-reduce a CPU tensor.
377	        reserved_metric_keys = _EAGLE3_STRUCTURED_METRIC_KEYS | {"accuracy", "loss"}
378	        for key, value in out.metrics.items():
379	            if key in reserved_metric_keys:
380	                continue
381	            if isinstance(value, torch.Tensor):
382	                if value.numel() != 1:
383	                    continue
384	                scalar = value.detach().reshape(()).to(out.loss.device)
385	            elif isinstance(value, (int, float)) and not isinstance(value, bool):
386	                scalar = out.loss.detach().new_tensor(float(value))
387	            else:
388	                continue
389	            scalar_metrics[key] = scalar
390	        if stepped:
391	            scalar_metrics = _dp_mean_scalars(
392	                scalar_metrics,
393	                process_group=process_group,
394	            )
395	        metrics.update({key: _scalar(value) for key, value in scalar_metrics.items()})
396	        gn = _scalar(grad_norm) if grad_norm is not None else None
397	        if gn is not None:
398	            metrics["grad_norm"] = gn
399	        return StepResult(
400	            optimizer_stepped=stepped,
401	            loss=metrics["loss"],
402	            grad_norm=gn,
403	            metrics=metrics,
404	        )
405	
406	
407	class TrainerController:
408	    """Lifecycle: fit / evaluate / checkpoint.
409	
410	    ``save_checkpoint`` persists resumable draft state and returns a
411	    :class:`Checkpoint`; ``specforge export`` materializes that state into the
412	    serving or Hugging Face model format.  Evaluation is configured once at
413	    construction time, so the public training lifecycle remains one no-argument
414	    :meth:`Trainer.fit` call.
415	    """
416	
417	    def __init__(
418	        self,
419	        core: TrainerCore,
420	        *,
421	        run_id: str,
422	        output_dir: str = "./output",
423	        save_interval: int = 0,
424	        eval_interval: int = 0,
425	        eval_data_factory: Optional[
426	            Callable[[], Optional[Iterable[TrainBatch]]]
427	        ] = None,
428	        log_interval: int = 50,
429	        max_steps: Optional[int] = None,
430	        total_steps: Optional[int] = None,
431	        num_epochs: int = 1,
432	        logger: Optional[Callable[[Dict[str, Any], int], None]] = None,
433	        ack_fn: Optional[Callable[[List[str], int], None]] = None,
434	        start_step: int = 0,
435	        start_epoch: int = 0,
436	        start_batch: int = 0,
437	        start_samples: int = 0,
438	        data_prepositioned: bool = False,
439	        checkpoint_manager: Optional[Any] = None,
440	        checkpoint_extra: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
441	        profiling_options=None,
442	    ) -> None:
443	        if (start_batch == 0) != (start_samples == 0):
444	            raise ValueError(
445	                f"start_batch={start_batch} and start_samples={start_samples} "
446	                f"describe the same mid-epoch position and must be zero or "
447	                f"nonzero together"
448	            )
449	        self.core = core
450	        self.run_id = run_id
451	        self.output_dir = output_dir
452	        self.save_interval = save_interval
453	        self.eval_interval = eval_interval
454	        self.eval_data_factory = eval_data_factory
455	        self.log_interval = log_interval
456	        # Injected manager (rotation, best metric) or the lazy default layout.
457	        self._checkpoint_mgr = checkpoint_manager
458	        # Extra entries merged into the shared checkpoint payload at save
459	        # (e.g. dataset_size / accumulation_steps, validated on resume).
460	        self.checkpoint_extra = dict(checkpoint_extra or {})
461	        self.max_steps = max_steps
462	        # Schedule horizon for step-dependent losses (Domino's lambda_base decay);
463	        # distinct from max_steps, an optional early-stop CAP. Falls back to
464	        # max_steps; None means schedule-reading strategies decay nothing.
465	        self.total_steps = total_steps if total_steps is not None else max_steps
466	        self.num_epochs = num_epochs
467	        self.logger = logger
468	        # ack_fn(sample_ids, global_step) records the durable ack transaction at
469	        # the optimizer-step boundary; None = the loader acks (simple runs).
470	        self.ack_fn = ack_fn
471	        # global_step counts OPTIMIZER steps (increments only at a grad-accum
472	        # boundary) so ack/checkpoint/resume semantics are in true optimizer
473	        # steps; micro_step counts forward/backward micro-batches.
474	        self.global_step = start_step
475	        self.micro_step = 0
476	        self.epoch = start_epoch
477	        # Live position within the current epoch, in batches and in SAMPLES
478	        # (batch-size independent, the persisted form). Nonzero at an epoch start
479	        # — seeded here on resume, or left over from a mid-epoch max_steps return
480	        # — makes fit() skip that prefix instead of re-training it.
481	        self._epoch_batch = start_batch
482	        self._epoch_samples = start_samples
483	        # A resumed online queue is rebuilt from the deterministic prompt plan
484	        # with its trained prefix already removed. Suppress the generic iterable
485	        # seek exactly once; consuming ``start_batch`` again would skip fresh data.
486	        self._data_prepositioned = bool(data_prepositioned)
487	        self.last_metrics: Dict[str, Any] = {}
488	        self.last_checkpoint_step: Optional[int] = None
489	        from specforge.training.profiling import ProfilingOptions, StepProfiler
490	
491	        options = profiling_options or ProfilingOptions()
492	        self._step_profiler = StepProfiler(
493	            options,
494	            output_dir=output_dir,
495	        )
496	
497	    def _make_progress_bar(self):
498	        """Build a rank-0 optimizer-step bar for interactive terminals only."""
499	        if not sys.stderr.isatty() or (
500	            self.max_steps is not None and self.global_step >= self.max_steps
501	        ):
502	            return None
503	        import torch.distributed as dist
504	
505	        if dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized() and dist.get_rank() != 0:
506	            return None
507	        from tqdm import tqdm
508	
509	        total = self.max_steps if self.max_steps is not None else self.total_steps
510	        return tqdm(
511	            total=total,
512	            initial=self.global_step,
513	            desc="Training",
514	            unit="step",
515	            mininterval=1.0,
516	            dynamic_ncols=True,
517	        )
518	
519	    def fit(self, data: Iterable[TrainBatch]) -> int:
520	        progress = self._make_progress_bar()
521	        try:
522	            return self._fit(data, progress)
523	        finally:
524	            if progress is not None:
525	                progress.close()
526	
527	    def _fit(self, data: Iterable[TrainBatch], progress: Optional[Any]) -> int:
528	        if self.max_steps is not None and self.global_step >= self.max_steps:
529	            logger.info(
530	                "fit: global_step=%d already at max_steps=%d; nothing to train",
531	                self.global_step,
532	                self.max_steps,
533	            )
534	            return self.global_step
535	        module = self.core.strategy.trainable_module()
536	        module.train()
537	        # Rank0-broadcast once: rank-local assembly must not let ranks enter or
538	        # skip the evaluator's collectives independently.
539	        eval_enabled = self._rank0_decision(
540	            self.eval_interval > 0 and self.eval_data_factory is not None
541	        )
542	        pending_ack: List[str] = []
543	        for epoch in range(self.epoch, self.num_epochs):
544	            self.epoch = epoch
545	            if hasattr(data, "set_epoch"):
546	                data.set_epoch(epoch)
547	            stream: Iterable[TrainBatch] = data
548	            skip = self._epoch_batch
549	            if skip:
550	                if self._data_prepositioned:
551	                    self._data_prepositioned = False
552	                elif hasattr(data, "seek"):
553	                    data.seek(skip)
554	                else:
555	                    it = iter(data)
556	                    consumed = sum(1 for _ in itertools.islice(it, skip))
557	                    if consumed < skip:
558	                        raise ValueError(
559	                            f"resume position skips past the end of the data: "
560	                            f"epoch {epoch} yielded only {consumed} batches, "
561	                            f"cannot skip {skip}"
562	                        )
563	                    stream = it
564	            _it = iter(stream)
565	            while True:
566	                try:
567	                    batch = next(_it)
568	                except StopIteration:
569	                    break
570	                self._epoch_batch += 1
571	                self._epoch_samples += len(batch.sample_ids)
572	                self.micro_step += 1
573	                if self.ack_fn is not None:
574	                    pending_ack.extend(batch.sample_ids)
575	                self._step_profiler.before_micro_step(self.global_step)
576	                result = self.core.train_step(
577	                    batch,
578	                    ctx=StepContext(
579	                        global_step=self.global_step, total_steps=self.total_steps
580	                    ),
581	                )
582	                self.last_metrics = result.metrics
583	                # grad accumulated but optimizer has not stepped yet; everything
584	                # keyed on optimizer steps fires only at the boundary.
585	                if not result.optimizer_stepped:
586	                    continue
587	                self.global_step += 1
588	                self._step_profiler.after_optimizer_step(self.global_step)
589	                if self.ack_fn is not None:
590	                    # durable ack transaction at the optimizer-step boundary
591	                    self.ack_fn(pending_ack, self.global_step)
592	                    pending_ack = []
593	                if self.logger and self.global_step % max(1, self.log_interval) == 0:
594	                    log_metrics = dict(result.metrics)
595	                    optimizer = getattr(self.core.backend, "optimizer", None)
596	                    get_learning_rate = getattr(optimizer, "get_learning_rate", None)
597	                    if callable(get_learning_rate):
598	                        log_metrics["lr"] = float(get_learning_rate())
599	                    self.logger(log_metrics, self.global_step)
600	                eval_metrics: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
601	                if eval_enabled and self.global_step % self.eval_interval == 0:
602	                    eval_metrics = self.evaluate_configured()
603	                    module.train()
604	                    if eval_metrics:
605	                        if self.logger:
606	                            self.logger(eval_metrics, self.global_step)
607	                        self.last_metrics = {**self.last_metrics, **eval_metrics}
608	                # ``is_better`` is collective (rank0 verdict broadcast inside
609	                # the manager); its guard is rank-identical because eval metrics
610	                # are DP-reduced. Empty eval metrics skip best tracking.
611	                interval_hit = bool(
612	                    self.save_interval and self.global_step % self.save_interval == 0
613	                )
614	                is_best = bool(
615	                    eval_metrics and self._checkpoint_manager().is_better(eval_metrics)
616	                )
617	                if interval_hit or is_best:
618	                    self.save_checkpoint(self.global_step)
619	                if is_best:
620	                    self._checkpoint_manager().update_best(
621	                        self.global_step, eval_metrics
622	                    )
623	                if progress is not None:
624	                    progress.update(1)
625	                if self.max_steps is not None and self.global_step >= self.max_steps:
626	                    return self.global_step
627	            self._epoch_batch = 0
628	            self._epoch_samples = 0
629	            # Persist the *next* epoch after a naturally exhausted pass.  A
630	            # checkpoint taken after fit() returns must describe completed
631	            # work, not epoch ``N`` at batch zero (which would replay that
632	            # entire epoch on resume).
633	            self.epoch = epoch + 1
634	        remainder = self.core.accumulation_remainder
635	        if remainder:
636	            raise RuntimeError(
637	                "training stream ended with incomplete gradient accumulation: "
638	                f"received {remainder} of {self.core.accumulation_steps} "
639	                "micro-batches after the last optimizer step; no partial "
640	                "optimizer step or durable acknowledgement was committed"
641	            )
642	        return self.global_step
643	
644	    def close_profiler(self) -> None:
645	        """Finalize a partial profiling window on every training exit path."""
646	        try:
647	            self._step_profiler.close(self.global_step)
648	        except Exception:
649	            logger.exception("failed to finalize the training profiler")
650	
651	    def evaluate_configured(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
652	        """Build one fresh eval pass and close any managed capture stream.
653	
654	        Fixed offline loaders may simply be returned on every call. Online eval
655	        factories can return an iterable context manager so each interval gets
656	        a fresh rollout stream without exposing an extra argument on ``fit``.
657	        """
658	        if self.eval_data_factory is None:
659	            return self.evaluate(None)
660	        data = self.eval_data_factory()
661	        if data is None or not hasattr(data, "__enter__"):
662	            return self.evaluate(data)
663	        with data as entered:
664	            return self.evaluate(data if entered is None else entered)
665	
666	    @torch.no_grad()
667	    def evaluate(self, data: Optional[Iterable[TrainBatch]]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
668	        """Full-pass eval via :class:`Evaluator`.
669	
670	        Returns rank-identical ``eval/*`` metrics, or ``{}`` when zero batches
671	        were processed globally. ``data=None`` (an empty local shard) still
672	        joins the evaluator's collectives.
673	        """
674	        from specforge.eval import Evaluator
675	
676	        module = self.core.strategy.trainable_module()
677	        was_training = module.training
678	        module.eval()
679	        # Use the train path's live context so schedule-dependent losses (for
680	        # example Domino's lambda_base) are not evaluated as if at step zero.
681	        ctx = StepContext(global_step=self.global_step, total_steps=self.total_steps)
682	        try:
683	            return Evaluator().run(
684	                lambda batch: self.core.strategy.forward_loss(batch, ctx), data
685	            )
686	        finally:
687	            module.train(was_training)
688	
689	    @staticmethod
690	    def _rank0_decision(flag: bool) -> bool:
691	        """Broadcast rank0's verdict for a collective-bearing branch."""
692	        if (
693	            not torch.distributed.is_available()
694	            or not torch.distributed.is_initialized()
695	            or torch.distributed.get_world_size() == 1
696	        ):
697	            return bool(flag)
698	        box = [bool(flag)] if torch.distributed.get_rank() == 0 else [False]
699	        torch.distributed.broadcast_object_list(box, src=0)
700	        return bool(box[0])
701	
702	    def _checkpoint_manager(self):
703	        # Lazily built in its S-home so the runtime seam does not import the domain
704	        # layer at module load (mirrors _assemble_trainer's lazy Trainer import).
705	        if self._checkpoint_mgr is None:
706	            from specforge.training.checkpoint import CheckpointManager
707	
708	            self._checkpoint_mgr = CheckpointManager(self.output_dir, self.run_id)
709	        return self._checkpoint_mgr
710	
711	    def save_checkpoint(self, step: int) -> Checkpoint:
712	        # Every rank participates: FSDP model gathering is collective and every
713	        # rank persists its RNG. Sharded optimizer state stays rank-local; the
714	        # identical DDP optimizer is written once in the shared rank0 payload.
715	        full = self.core.backend.state_dict()
716	        mgr = self._checkpoint_manager()
717	        replicated_optimizer = bool(
718	            getattr(self.core.backend, "optimizer_state_is_replicated", False)
719	        )
720	        shared = None
721	        if mgr.is_rank0():
722	            shared = {
723	                "draft_state_dict": self.core.strategy.checkpoint_state_filter(
724	                    full["model"]
725	                ),
726	                "global_step": step,
727	                "epoch": self.epoch,
728	                "epoch_batch": self._epoch_batch,
729	                "epoch_samples": self._epoch_samples,
730	                "strategy": self.core.strategy.name,
731	                "run_id": self.run_id,
732	                "world_size": (
733	                    torch.distributed.get_world_size()
734	                    if torch.distributed.is_initialized()
735	                    else 1
736	                ),
737	                **self.checkpoint_extra,
738	            }
739	            if replicated_optimizer:
740	                # DDP ranks have identical parameters, gradients, optimizer
741	                # moments, and scheduler state. Persist that large state once;
742	                # rank files still preserve their distinct RNG streams.
743	                shared["replicated_optimizer_state"] = full["optimizer"]
744	        ckpt_dir = mgr.save(
745	            shared,
746	            step,
747	            rank_state={
748	                "optimizer": None if replicated_optimizer else full["optimizer"],
749	                "rng": full["rng"],
750	            },
751	        )
752	        self.last_checkpoint_step = step
753	        return Checkpoint(
754	            checkpoint_uri=f"file://{os.path.abspath(ckpt_dir)}",
755	            global_step=step,
756	            epoch=self.epoch,
757	            strategy=self.core.strategy.name,
758	        )
759	
760	
761	__all__ = ["TrainerCore", "TrainerController", "Checkpoint", "StepResult"]

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.

# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
"""TrainerCore + TrainerController: the trainer-boundary split.

``TrainerCore`` runs exactly one branch-free step (strategy forward/loss, backend
backward/step) plus the grad-accumulation boundary. ``TrainerController`` owns
the lifecycle: fit / evaluate / save_checkpoint. EAGLE3 and DFlash share this
unchanged — only the strategy differs.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import itertools
import logging
import os
import sys
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional

import torch

from specforge.runtime.contracts import TrainBatch
from specforge.training.backend import TrainingBackend
from specforge.training.strategies.base import (
    DraftTrainStrategy,
    StepContext,
    StepOutput,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Checkpoint:
    """A saved training checkpoint location (resume target) — deliberately NOT a
    published weight version (weight publication is not implemented)."""

    checkpoint_uri: str
    global_step: int
    epoch: int
    strategy: str
    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class StepResult:
    """Result of one TrainerCore step; ``optimizer_stepped`` is the authoritative
    grad-accumulation boundary signal."""

    optimizer_stepped: bool
    loss: float
    grad_norm: Optional[float]
    metrics: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


def _scalar(x: Any) -> float:
    if isinstance(x, torch.Tensor):
        return float(x.detach().float().mean().item())
    if isinstance(x, (list, tuple)) and x:
        return float(torch.stack([t.detach().float() for t in x]).mean().item())
    return float(x)


def _dp_mean_scalars(
    values: Dict[str, torch.Tensor], *, process_group: Any = None
) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    """Average scalar metrics across DP ranks with one collective.

    Uses the established DFlash metric convention (DP mean):
    without it the disagg consumer logs a single rank's local-batch accuracy
    (~1 rank x batch x anchors), which is ~sqrt(world) noisier and spikes because
    each rank's few round-robin refs can be all-easy or all-hard. Reducing across
    ranks recovers the ~world x larger effective sample the stock path logs.
    The caller invokes this only at an optimizer boundary: intermediate
    micro-step metrics are not logged, so synchronizing them only adds latency.
    """
    import torch.distributed as dist

    if not values or not (dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized()):
        return values
    world = (
        dist.get_world_size()
        if process_group is None
        else dist.get_world_size(group=process_group)
    )
    if world <= 1:
        return values
    names = list(values)
    packed = torch.stack([values[name].detach().float().reshape(()) for name in names])
    if process_group is None:
        dist.all_reduce(packed)
    else:
        dist.all_reduce(packed, group=process_group)
    packed /= world
    return {name: packed[index] for index, name in enumerate(names)}


def _reduce_ratio_metrics(
    values: Dict[str, Any],
    *,
    device: torch.device,
    process_group: Any,
    reduce: bool,
) -> Dict[str, float]:
    """Form telemetry ratios only after summing their numerators and counts."""

    if not values:
        return {}
    normalized = []
    for name in sorted(values):
        pair = values[name]
        if not isinstance(pair, (tuple, list)) or len(pair) != 2:
            raise TypeError(
                f"ratio metric {name!r} must be a (numerator, denominator) pair"
            )
        numerator = torch.as_tensor(pair[0]).detach().float().flatten().to(device)
        denominator = torch.as_tensor(pair[1]).detach().float().flatten().to(device)
        if numerator.shape != denominator.shape:
            raise ValueError(
                f"ratio metric {name!r} shape mismatch: "
                f"{tuple(numerator.shape)} vs {tuple(denominator.shape)}"
            )
        normalized.append((name, numerator, denominator))

    packed = torch.cat(
        [
            tensor
            for _, numerator, denominator in normalized
            for tensor in (numerator, denominator)
        ]
    )
    if reduce:
        import torch.distributed as dist

        if dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized():
            if process_group is None:
                dist.all_reduce(packed)
            else:
                dist.all_reduce(packed, group=process_group)

    output: Dict[str, float] = {}
    cursor = 0
    for name, numerator, _denominator in normalized:
        width = numerator.numel()
        summed_numerator = packed[cursor : cursor + width]
        cursor += width
        summed_denominator = packed[cursor : cursor + width]
        cursor += width
        ratios = summed_numerator / summed_denominator.clamp_min(1e-12)
        if width == 1:
            output[name] = float(ratios.item())
        else:
            output.update(
                {f"{name}_{index}": float(value) for index, value in enumerate(ratios)}
            )
    return output


_EAGLE3_STRUCTURED_METRIC_KEYS = frozenset(
    {
        "acces",
        "acceptance_rates",
        "plosses",
        "acc_corrects",
        "acc_denoms",
        "metric_losses",
        "metric_loss_denoms",
    }
)


def _metric_vector(values: Any, *, device: torch.device, name: str) -> torch.Tensor:
    """Normalize one per-TTT metric sequence without losing its positions."""
    if isinstance(values, torch.Tensor):
        vector = values.detach().flatten()
    elif isinstance(values, (list, tuple)):
        vector = torch.stack(
            [torch.as_tensor(value).detach().reshape(()) for value in values]
        )
    else:
        raise TypeError(f"{name} must be a tensor or sequence, got {type(values)!r}")
    if vector.numel() == 0:
        raise ValueError(f"{name} must contain at least one TTT position")
    return vector.to(device=device, dtype=torch.float32)


def _reduce_eagle3_metrics(
    raw: Dict[str, Any],
    *,
    device: torch.device,
    process_group: Any,
    ploss_decay: float,
) -> Optional[Dict[str, float]]:
    """Reduce EAGLE3's per-position training telemetry as numerators/counts.

    Accuracy and p-loss are ratios, so averaging rank-local ratios biases the
    result whenever ranks carry different token counts.  Pack every numerator
    and denominator into one collective and form ratios only after the global
    SUM.  Acceptance rate has no separate count in the model contract; weight
    it by the corresponding p-loss token count, matching the evaluator's
    batch-size-invariant convention.
    """
    required = {
        "acc_corrects",
        "acc_denoms",
        "metric_losses",
        "metric_loss_denoms",
    }
    if not required.issubset(raw):
        return None

    corrects = _metric_vector(raw["acc_corrects"], device=device, name="acc_corrects")
    acc_denoms = _metric_vector(raw["acc_denoms"], device=device, name="acc_denoms")
    losses = _metric_vector(raw["metric_losses"], device=device, name="metric_losses")
    loss_denoms = _metric_vector(
        raw["metric_loss_denoms"], device=device, name="metric_loss_denoms"
    )
    length = corrects.numel()
    vectors = {
        "acc_denoms": acc_denoms,
        "metric_losses": losses,
        "metric_loss_denoms": loss_denoms,
    }
    acceptance_rates = None
    if "acceptance_rates" in raw:
        acceptance_rates = _metric_vector(
            raw["acceptance_rates"], device=device, name="acceptance_rates"
        )
        vectors["acceptance_rates"] = acceptance_rates
    mismatched = {
        name: value.numel()
        for name, value in vectors.items()
        if value.numel() != length
    }
    if mismatched:
        raise ValueError(
            "EAGLE3 structured metric lengths must match acc_corrects "
            f"({length}); got {mismatched}"
        )

    # Rows: accuracy numerator/denominator, p-loss numerator/denominator,
    # acceptance numerator/denominator.  The last two rows stay zero when the
    # strategy does not expose acceptance telemetry.
    packed = torch.stack(
        (
            corrects,
            acc_denoms,
            losses * loss_denoms,
            loss_denoms,
            (
                acceptance_rates * loss_denoms
                if acceptance_rates is not None
                else torch.zeros_like(loss_denoms)
            ),
            (
                loss_denoms
                if acceptance_rates is not None
                else torch.zeros_like(loss_denoms)
            ),
        )
    )

    import torch.distributed as dist

    if dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized():
        world = dist.get_world_size(process_group)
        if world > 1:
            dist.all_reduce(packed, op=dist.ReduceOp.SUM, group=process_group)

    reduced_acc = packed[0] / packed[1].clamp_min(1e-6)
    reduced_ploss = packed[2] / packed[3].clamp_min(1e-6)
    result: Dict[str, float] = {}
    for index, value in enumerate(reduced_acc.tolist()):
        result[f"acc_{index}"] = float(value)
    for index, value in enumerate(reduced_ploss.tolist()):
        result[f"ploss_{index}"] = float(value)

    result["acc"] = float(packed[0].sum().div(packed[1].sum().clamp_min(1e-6)).item())
    weights = torch.tensor(
        [ploss_decay**index for index in range(length)],
        dtype=reduced_ploss.dtype,
        device=reduced_ploss.device,
    )
    result["loss"] = float((reduced_ploss * weights).sum().item())

    if acceptance_rates is not None:
        reduced_acceptance = packed[4] / packed[5].clamp_min(1e-6)
        for index, value in enumerate(reduced_acceptance.tolist()):
            result[f"acceptance_rate_{index}"] = float(value)
        result["acceptance_rate"] = float(reduced_acceptance.mean().item())
    return result


class TrainerCore:
    """One step: forward/loss (strategy) -> backward (backend) -> optimizer boundary."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        strategy: DraftTrainStrategy,
        backend: TrainingBackend,
        *,
        accumulation_steps: int = 1,
    ) -> None:
        self.strategy = strategy
        self.backend = backend
        self.accumulation_steps = max(1, accumulation_steps)
        self._micro = 0

    @property
    def accumulation_remainder(self) -> int:
        """Micro-batches whose gradients have not reached an optimizer step."""
        return self._micro % self.accumulation_steps

    def train_step(
        self, batch: TrainBatch, ctx: Optional[StepContext] = None
    ) -> StepResult:
        out: StepOutput = self.strategy.forward_loss(batch, ctx)
        loss = out.loss / self.accumulation_steps
        self._micro += 1
        # The boundary is known before backward so the backend can defer the FSDP
        # gradient reduction (no_sync) on non-boundary micro-steps.
        stepped = self._micro % self.accumulation_steps == 0
        self.backend.backward(loss, is_boundary=stepped)
        grad_norm = self.backend.step() if stepped else None
        return self._result(out, grad_norm, stepped)

    def _result(self, out: StepOutput, grad_norm, stepped: bool) -> StepResult:
        # EAGLE3 carries per-TTT numerators and denominators.  Preserve those
        # positions and reduce counts before ratios; scalarizing its lists here
        # would both collapse the TTT structure and log one rank's local data.
        metric_device = (
            out.loss.device
            if isinstance(out.loss, torch.Tensor)
            else torch.device("cpu")
        )
        parallel_config = getattr(self.backend, "parallel_config", None)
        process_group = getattr(parallel_config, "fsdp_process_group", None)
        structured = _reduce_eagle3_metrics(
            out.metrics,
            device=metric_device,
            process_group=process_group,
            ploss_decay=float(getattr(self.strategy, "ploss_decay", 1.0)),
        )
        # Structured EAGLE3 metrics are already globally reduced.  Remaining
        # scalar diagnostics are DP-averaged in a single collective at optimizer
        # boundaries; non-boundary results stay rank-local.
        metrics: Dict[str, Any] = dict(structured or {})
        metrics.update(
            _reduce_ratio_metrics(
                out.ratio_metrics,
                device=metric_device,
                process_group=process_group,
                reduce=stepped,
            )
        )
        scalar_metrics: Dict[str, torch.Tensor] = {}
        if "loss" not in metrics:
            scalar_metrics["loss"] = out.loss
        if "accuracy" in out.metrics and "acc" not in metrics:
            accuracy = out.metrics["accuracy"]
            if isinstance(accuracy, torch.Tensor):
                scalar_metrics["acc"] = (
                    accuracy.detach().float().mean().to(out.loss.device)
                )
            elif isinstance(accuracy, (int, float)) and not isinstance(accuracy, bool):
                scalar_metrics["acc"] = out.loss.detach().new_tensor(float(accuracy))
        # Strategies may expose additional scalar diagnostics without teaching
        # the generic trainer their algorithm-specific names. Move CPU schedule
        # scalars (for example Domino's lambda_base) onto the loss device before
        # the DP reduction so NCCL-backed runs do not all-reduce a CPU tensor.
        reserved_metric_keys = _EAGLE3_STRUCTURED_METRIC_KEYS | {"accuracy", "loss"}
        for key, value in out.metrics.items():
            if key in reserved_metric_keys:
                continue
            if isinstance(value, torch.Tensor):
                if value.numel() != 1:
                    continue
                scalar = value.detach().reshape(()).to(out.loss.device)
            elif isinstance(value, (int, float)) and not isinstance(value, bool):
                scalar = out.loss.detach().new_tensor(float(value))
            else:
                continue
            scalar_metrics[key] = scalar
        if stepped:
            scalar_metrics = _dp_mean_scalars(
                scalar_metrics,
                process_group=process_group,
            )
        metrics.update({key: _scalar(value) for key, value in scalar_metrics.items()})
        gn = _scalar(grad_norm) if grad_norm is not None else None
        if gn is not None:
            metrics["grad_norm"] = gn
        return StepResult(
            optimizer_stepped=stepped,
            loss=metrics["loss"],
            grad_norm=gn,
            metrics=metrics,
        )


class TrainerController:
    """Lifecycle: fit / evaluate / checkpoint.

    ``save_checkpoint`` persists resumable draft state and returns a
    :class:`Checkpoint`; ``specforge export`` materializes that state into the
    serving or Hugging Face model format.  Evaluation is configured once at
    construction time, so the public training lifecycle remains one no-argument
    :meth:`Trainer.fit` call.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        core: TrainerCore,
        *,
        run_id: str,
        output_dir: str = "./output",
        save_interval: int = 0,
        eval_interval: int = 0,
        eval_data_factory: Optional[
            Callable[[], Optional[Iterable[TrainBatch]]]
        ] = None,
        log_interval: int = 50,
        max_steps: Optional[int] = None,
        total_steps: Optional[int] = None,
        num_epochs: int = 1,
        logger: Optional[Callable[[Dict[str, Any], int], None]] = None,
        ack_fn: Optional[Callable[[List[str], int], None]] = None,
        start_step: int = 0,
        start_epoch: int = 0,
        start_batch: int = 0,
        start_samples: int = 0,
        data_prepositioned: bool = False,
        checkpoint_manager: Optional[Any] = None,
        checkpoint_extra: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        profiling_options=None,
    ) -> None:
        if (start_batch == 0) != (start_samples == 0):
            raise ValueError(
                f"start_batch={start_batch} and start_samples={start_samples} "
                f"describe the same mid-epoch position and must be zero or "
                f"nonzero together"
            )
        self.core = core
        self.run_id = run_id
        self.output_dir = output_dir
        self.save_interval = save_interval
        self.eval_interval = eval_interval
        self.eval_data_factory = eval_data_factory
        self.log_interval = log_interval
        # Injected manager (rotation, best metric) or the lazy default layout.
        self._checkpoint_mgr = checkpoint_manager
        # Extra entries merged into the shared checkpoint payload at save
        # (e.g. dataset_size / accumulation_steps, validated on resume).
        self.checkpoint_extra = dict(checkpoint_extra or {})
        self.max_steps = max_steps
        # Schedule horizon for step-dependent losses (Domino's lambda_base decay);
        # distinct from max_steps, an optional early-stop CAP. Falls back to
        # max_steps; None means schedule-reading strategies decay nothing.
        self.total_steps = total_steps if total_steps is not None else max_steps
        self.num_epochs = num_epochs
        self.logger = logger
        # ack_fn(sample_ids, global_step) records the durable ack transaction at
        # the optimizer-step boundary; None = the loader acks (simple runs).
        self.ack_fn = ack_fn
        # global_step counts OPTIMIZER steps (increments only at a grad-accum
        # boundary) so ack/checkpoint/resume semantics are in true optimizer
        # steps; micro_step counts forward/backward micro-batches.
        self.global_step = start_step
        self.micro_step = 0
        self.epoch = start_epoch
        # Live position within the current epoch, in batches and in SAMPLES
        # (batch-size independent, the persisted form). Nonzero at an epoch start
        # — seeded here on resume, or left over from a mid-epoch max_steps return
        # — makes fit() skip that prefix instead of re-training it.
        self._epoch_batch = start_batch
        self._epoch_samples = start_samples
        # A resumed online queue is rebuilt from the deterministic prompt plan
        # with its trained prefix already removed. Suppress the generic iterable
        # seek exactly once; consuming ``start_batch`` again would skip fresh data.
        self._data_prepositioned = bool(data_prepositioned)
        self.last_metrics: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        self.last_checkpoint_step: Optional[int] = None
        from specforge.training.profiling import ProfilingOptions, StepProfiler

        options = profiling_options or ProfilingOptions()
        self._step_profiler = StepProfiler(
            options,
            output_dir=output_dir,
        )

    def _make_progress_bar(self):
        """Build a rank-0 optimizer-step bar for interactive terminals only."""
        if not sys.stderr.isatty() or (
            self.max_steps is not None and self.global_step >= self.max_steps
        ):
            return None
        import torch.distributed as dist

        if dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized() and dist.get_rank() != 0:
            return None
        from tqdm import tqdm

        total = self.max_steps if self.max_steps is not None else self.total_steps
        return tqdm(
            total=total,
            initial=self.global_step,
            desc="Training",
            unit="step",
            mininterval=1.0,
            dynamic_ncols=True,
        )

    def fit(self, data: Iterable[TrainBatch]) -> int:
        progress = self._make_progress_bar()
        try:
            return self._fit(data, progress)
        finally:
            if progress is not None:
                progress.close()

    def _fit(self, data: Iterable[TrainBatch], progress: Optional[Any]) -> int:
        if self.max_steps is not None and self.global_step >= self.max_steps:
            logger.info(
                "fit: global_step=%d already at max_steps=%d; nothing to train",
                self.global_step,
                self.max_steps,
            )
            return self.global_step
        module = self.core.strategy.trainable_module()
        module.train()
        # Rank0-broadcast once: rank-local assembly must not let ranks enter or
        # skip the evaluator's collectives independently.
        eval_enabled = self._rank0_decision(
            self.eval_interval > 0 and self.eval_data_factory is not None
        )
        pending_ack: List[str] = []
        for epoch in range(self.epoch, self.num_epochs):
            self.epoch = epoch
            if hasattr(data, "set_epoch"):
                data.set_epoch(epoch)
            stream: Iterable[TrainBatch] = data
            skip = self._epoch_batch
            if skip:
                if self._data_prepositioned:
                    self._data_prepositioned = False
                elif hasattr(data, "seek"):
                    data.seek(skip)
                else:
                    it = iter(data)
                    consumed = sum(1 for _ in itertools.islice(it, skip))
                    if consumed < skip:
                        raise ValueError(
                            f"resume position skips past the end of the data: "
                            f"epoch {epoch} yielded only {consumed} batches, "
                            f"cannot skip {skip}"
                        )
                    stream = it
            _it = iter(stream)
            while True:
                try:
                    batch = next(_it)
                except StopIteration:
                    break
                self._epoch_batch += 1
                self._epoch_samples += len(batch.sample_ids)
                self.micro_step += 1
                if self.ack_fn is not None:
                    pending_ack.extend(batch.sample_ids)
                self._step_profiler.before_micro_step(self.global_step)
                result = self.core.train_step(
                    batch,
                    ctx=StepContext(
                        global_step=self.global_step, total_steps=self.total_steps
                    ),
                )
                self.last_metrics = result.metrics
                # grad accumulated but optimizer has not stepped yet; everything
                # keyed on optimizer steps fires only at the boundary.
                if not result.optimizer_stepped:
                    continue
                self.global_step += 1
                self._step_profiler.after_optimizer_step(self.global_step)
                if self.ack_fn is not None:
                    # durable ack transaction at the optimizer-step boundary
                    self.ack_fn(pending_ack, self.global_step)
                    pending_ack = []
                if self.logger and self.global_step % max(1, self.log_interval) == 0:
                    log_metrics = dict(result.metrics)
                    optimizer = getattr(self.core.backend, "optimizer", None)
                    get_learning_rate = getattr(optimizer, "get_learning_rate", None)
                    if callable(get_learning_rate):
                        log_metrics["lr"] = float(get_learning_rate())
                    self.logger(log_metrics, self.global_step)
                eval_metrics: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
                if eval_enabled and self.global_step % self.eval_interval == 0:
                    eval_metrics = self.evaluate_configured()
                    module.train()
                    if eval_metrics:
                        if self.logger:
                            self.logger(eval_metrics, self.global_step)
                        self.last_metrics = {**self.last_metrics, **eval_metrics}
                # ``is_better`` is collective (rank0 verdict broadcast inside
                # the manager); its guard is rank-identical because eval metrics
                # are DP-reduced. Empty eval metrics skip best tracking.
                interval_hit = bool(
                    self.save_interval and self.global_step % self.save_interval == 0
                )
                is_best = bool(
                    eval_metrics and self._checkpoint_manager().is_better(eval_metrics)
                )
                if interval_hit or is_best:
                    self.save_checkpoint(self.global_step)
                if is_best:
                    self._checkpoint_manager().update_best(
                        self.global_step, eval_metrics
                    )
                if progress is not None:
                    progress.update(1)
                if self.max_steps is not None and self.global_step >= self.max_steps:
                    return self.global_step
            self._epoch_batch = 0
            self._epoch_samples = 0
            # Persist the *next* epoch after a naturally exhausted pass.  A
            # checkpoint taken after fit() returns must describe completed
            # work, not epoch ``N`` at batch zero (which would replay that
            # entire epoch on resume).
            self.epoch = epoch + 1
        remainder = self.core.accumulation_remainder
        if remainder:
            raise RuntimeError(
                "training stream ended with incomplete gradient accumulation: "
                f"received {remainder} of {self.core.accumulation_steps} "
                "micro-batches after the last optimizer step; no partial "
                "optimizer step or durable acknowledgement was committed"
            )
        return self.global_step

    def close_profiler(self) -> None:
        """Finalize a partial profiling window on every training exit path."""
        try:
            self._step_profiler.close(self.global_step)
        except Exception:
            logger.exception("failed to finalize the training profiler")

    def evaluate_configured(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Build one fresh eval pass and close any managed capture stream.

        Fixed offline loaders may simply be returned on every call. Online eval
        factories can return an iterable context manager so each interval gets
        a fresh rollout stream without exposing an extra argument on ``fit``.
        """
        if self.eval_data_factory is None:
            return self.evaluate(None)
        data = self.eval_data_factory()
        if data is None or not hasattr(data, "__enter__"):
            return self.evaluate(data)
        with data as entered:
            return self.evaluate(data if entered is None else entered)

    @torch.no_grad()
    def evaluate(self, data: Optional[Iterable[TrainBatch]]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Full-pass eval via :class:`Evaluator`.

        Returns rank-identical ``eval/*`` metrics, or ``{}`` when zero batches
        were processed globally. ``data=None`` (an empty local shard) still
        joins the evaluator's collectives.
        """
        from specforge.eval import Evaluator

        module = self.core.strategy.trainable_module()
        was_training = module.training
        module.eval()
        # Use the train path's live context so schedule-dependent losses (for
        # example Domino's lambda_base) are not evaluated as if at step zero.
        ctx = StepContext(global_step=self.global_step, total_steps=self.total_steps)
        try:
            return Evaluator().run(
                lambda batch: self.core.strategy.forward_loss(batch, ctx), data
            )
        finally:
            module.train(was_training)

    @staticmethod
    def _rank0_decision(flag: bool) -> bool:
        """Broadcast rank0's verdict for a collective-bearing branch."""
        if (
            not torch.distributed.is_available()
            or not torch.distributed.is_initialized()
            or torch.distributed.get_world_size() == 1
        ):
            return bool(flag)
        box = [bool(flag)] if torch.distributed.get_rank() == 0 else [False]
        torch.distributed.broadcast_object_list(box, src=0)
        return bool(box[0])

    def _checkpoint_manager(self):
        # Lazily built in its S-home so the runtime seam does not import the domain
        # layer at module load (mirrors _assemble_trainer's lazy Trainer import).
        if self._checkpoint_mgr is None:
            from specforge.training.checkpoint import CheckpointManager

            self._checkpoint_mgr = CheckpointManager(self.output_dir, self.run_id)
        return self._checkpoint_mgr

    def save_checkpoint(self, step: int) -> Checkpoint:
        # Every rank participates: FSDP model gathering is collective and every
        # rank persists its RNG. Sharded optimizer state stays rank-local; the
        # identical DDP optimizer is written once in the shared rank0 payload.
        full = self.core.backend.state_dict()
        mgr = self._checkpoint_manager()
        replicated_optimizer = bool(
            getattr(self.core.backend, "optimizer_state_is_replicated", False)
        )
        shared = None
        if mgr.is_rank0():
            shared = {
                "draft_state_dict": self.core.strategy.checkpoint_state_filter(
                    full["model"]
                ),
                "global_step": step,
                "epoch": self.epoch,
                "epoch_batch": self._epoch_batch,
                "epoch_samples": self._epoch_samples,
                "strategy": self.core.strategy.name,
                "run_id": self.run_id,
                "world_size": (
                    torch.distributed.get_world_size()
                    if torch.distributed.is_initialized()
                    else 1
                ),
                **self.checkpoint_extra,
            }
            if replicated_optimizer:
                # DDP ranks have identical parameters, gradients, optimizer
                # moments, and scheduler state. Persist that large state once;
                # rank files still preserve their distinct RNG streams.
                shared["replicated_optimizer_state"] = full["optimizer"]
        ckpt_dir = mgr.save(
            shared,
            step,
            rank_state={
                "optimizer": None if replicated_optimizer else full["optimizer"],
                "rng": full["rng"],
            },
        )
        self.last_checkpoint_step = step
        return Checkpoint(
            checkpoint_uri=f"file://{os.path.abspath(ckpt_dir)}",
            global_step=step,
            epoch=self.epoch,
            strategy=self.core.strategy.name,
        )


__all__ = ["TrainerCore", "TrainerController", "Checkpoint", "StepResult"]

import logging

import torch
import torch.distributed as dist

from specforge.lr_scheduler import CosineAnnealingWarmupLR
from specforge.utils import print_on_rank0

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class BF16Optimizer:
    """AdamW over fp32 master copies of the bf16 trainable params, with grad
    clipping and cosine warmup scheduling."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        model,
        lr,
        weight_decay=0.0,
        max_grad_norm=0.5,
        total_steps=800_000,
        warmup_ratio=0.015,
        offload_master=False,
    ):
        # defaults copied from EAGLE traineagle3 ds_config.json
        self.model = model
        self.model_params = [p for p in model.parameters() if p.requires_grad]
        self.max_grad_norm = max_grad_norm
        self.offload_master = bool(offload_master)
        self.fp32_params = [
            (
                p.detach().to(device="cpu", dtype=torch.float32).clone()
                if self.offload_master
                else p.detach().clone().to(torch.float32)
            )
            for p in self.model_params
        ]
        for mp in self.fp32_params:
            mp.requires_grad = True
        self.optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(
            self.fp32_params, lr=lr, weight_decay=weight_decay
        )
        self.last_grad_norm = None
        self._grad_norm_process_group = None
        self._reduce_grad_norm_across_ranks = True
        self.scheduler = CosineAnnealingWarmupLR(
            self.optimizer,
            total_steps=total_steps,
            warmup_steps=int(warmup_ratio * total_steps),
        )

    def configure_grad_norm_reduction(
        self, *, process_group=None, enabled: bool = True
    ) -> None:
        """Configure the group that owns disjoint gradient shards.

        FSDP backends disable the reduction for replicated/NO_SHARD parameters.
        """
        self._grad_norm_process_group = process_group
        self._reduce_grad_norm_across_ranks = enabled

    def _reduce_grad_norm(self, total_norm_sq):
        """All-reduce the squared L2 norm across shard ranks and derive the
        clip coefficient.

        ``total_norm_sq`` must already live on a device the process group can
        reduce (e.g. CUDA for NCCL). Returns ``(total_norm, clip_coef)``.
        """
        if (
            self._reduce_grad_norm_across_ranks
            and dist.is_available()
            and dist.is_initialized()
        ):
            dist.all_reduce(
                total_norm_sq,
                op=dist.ReduceOp.SUM,
                group=self._grad_norm_process_group,
            )
        total_norm = total_norm_sq.sqrt()
        clip_coef = torch.clamp(self.max_grad_norm / (total_norm + 1e-6), max=1.0)
        return total_norm, clip_coef

    def _grad_norm_and_clip_coefficient(self):
        """Compute the global grad norm from the model params on their own
        device, where NCCL can reduce it safely, without materialising master
        gradients first."""
        grads = [p.grad.detach() for p in self.model_params if p.grad is not None]
        if grads:
            total_norm_sq = torch.stack(
                [grad.float().square().sum() for grad in grads]
            ).sum()
        else:
            device = self.model_params[0].device if self.model_params else "cpu"
            total_norm_sq = torch.zeros((), dtype=torch.float32, device=device)
        return self._reduce_grad_norm(total_norm_sq)

    def _clip_grad_norm(self):
        """Clip already-populated FP32 master gradients in place.

        Convenience entry point for optimizer tests and custom loops. When
        masters are CPU-offloaded, only the scalar norm is moved to the model
        device so a NCCL process group can still participate in the reduction.
        """
        grads = [master.grad for master in self.fp32_params if master.grad is not None]
        if grads:
            local_norm_sq = torch.stack(
                [grad.float().square().sum() for grad in grads]
            ).sum()
        else:
            master_device = self.fp32_params[0].device if self.fp32_params else "cpu"
            local_norm_sq = torch.zeros((), dtype=torch.float32, device=master_device)

        reduction_device = (
            self.model_params[0].device if self.model_params else local_norm_sq.device
        )
        total_norm, clip_coef = self._reduce_grad_norm(
            local_norm_sq.to(reduction_device)
        )
        for grad in grads:
            coefficient = (
                clip_coef
                if clip_coef.device == grad.device
                else float(clip_coef.item())
            )
            grad.mul_(coefficient)
        return total_norm

    def step(self):
        grad_norm, clip_coefficient = self._grad_norm_and_clip_coefficient()
        cpu_clip_coefficient = (
            float(clip_coefficient.item()) if self.offload_master else None
        )
        with torch.no_grad():
            for p, mp in zip(self.model_params, self.fp32_params):
                if p.grad is None:
                    mp.grad = None
                    continue
                master_grad = p.grad.detach().to(
                    device=mp.device,
                    dtype=torch.float32,
                )
                master_grad.mul_(
                    cpu_clip_coefficient
                    if cpu_clip_coefficient is not None
                    else clip_coefficient
                )
                mp.grad = master_grad
        self.last_grad_norm = grad_norm.detach()
        self.optimizer.step()
        self.optimizer.zero_grad()
        self.scheduler.step()
        with torch.no_grad():
            for p, mp in zip(self.model_params, self.fp32_params):
                p.data.copy_(mp.data.to(device=p.device, dtype=p.dtype))
                p.grad = None
        return self.last_grad_norm

    def load_state_dict(self, state_dict):
        """Restore optimizer/scheduler state and, when present, the rank-local
        fp32 master params; without them the masters are re-cloned from the
        bf16 weights and the resume is not numerically faithful."""
        saved_max_grad_norm = state_dict.get("max_grad_norm")
        if saved_max_grad_norm is not None and float(saved_max_grad_norm) != float(
            self.max_grad_norm
        ):
            raise ValueError(
                "checkpoint optimizer used max_grad_norm="
                f"{saved_max_grad_norm} but this run has "
                f"max_grad_norm={self.max_grad_norm}"
            )
        # offload_master is a pure device-placement choice: restored fp32
        # masters and Adam moments are relocated to the current master device,
        # so toggling it on resume is safe and intentionally not gated here.
        self.optimizer.load_state_dict(state_dict["optimizer_state_dict"])
        print_on_rank0("Successfully loaded optimizer state_dict.")
        self.scheduler.load_state_dict(state_dict["scheduler_state_dict"])
        print_on_rank0("Successfully loaded scheduler state_dict.")
        saved_fp32 = state_dict.get("fp32_params")
        if saved_fp32 is not None:
            if len(saved_fp32) != len(self.fp32_params):
                raise ValueError(
                    f"checkpoint carries {len(saved_fp32)} fp32 master params "
                    f"but this rank has {len(self.fp32_params)}"
                )
            with torch.no_grad():
                for i, (saved, mp) in enumerate(zip(saved_fp32, self.fp32_params)):
                    if saved.shape != mp.shape:
                        raise ValueError(
                            f"fp32 master param {i} shape mismatch: checkpoint "
                            f"{tuple(saved.shape)} vs current {tuple(mp.shape)}"
                        )
                    mp.data.copy_(saved.to(mp.device, mp.dtype))
        else:
            logger.warning(
                "checkpoint has no fp32_params; re-cloning master params from "
                "bf16 weights — resume will not be numerically faithful"
            )
            with torch.no_grad():
                for p, mp in zip(self.model_params, self.fp32_params):
                    mp.data.copy_(p.detach().to(device=mp.device, dtype=mp.dtype))

    def state_dict(self):
        return {
            "optimizer_state_dict": self.optimizer.state_dict(),
            "scheduler_state_dict": self.scheduler.state_dict(),
            "max_grad_norm": self.max_grad_norm,
            # rank-local fp32 masters; without them a resume re-quantizes from bf16
            "fp32_params": [t.detach().cpu() for t in self.fp32_params],
        }

    def get_learning_rate(self):
        return self.optimizer.param_groups[0]["lr"]

68	    def trainable_module(self) -> nn.Module:

algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    *,
    logger=_logger,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    t = cfg.training
    # USP shards one logical sample over ``sp_size`` ranks.  Preserve the
    # legacy optimizer-window semantics: one user accumulation unit represents
    # a complete logical sequence, not one local sequence shard.
    accumulation_steps = t.accumulation_steps
    if t.attention_backend == "usp":
        accumulation_steps *= t.sp_ulysses_size * t.sp_ring_size
    return dict(
        algorithm=algorithm,
        modality=cfg.model.input_modality,
        optimizer_factory=_optimizer_factory(cfg),
        run_id=cfg.run_id,
        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
        batch_size=t.batch_size,
        accumulation_steps=accumulation_steps,
        max_steps=t.max_steps,
        total_steps=t.total_steps,
        save_interval=t.save_interval,
        eval_interval=t.eval_interval,
        max_checkpoints=t.max_checkpoints,
        logger=logger,

Let me think carefully about the key technical content to convey:

### FSDP wrapping (prepare_model)

- The `TrainingBackend` ABC defines 5 abstract methods: prepare_model, backward, step, state_dict, load_state_dict.
- `FSDPTrainingBackend` is the only implementation (FSDP-only for now).
- `prepare_model` decides between DDP and FSDP based on `sharding_strategy == "NO_SHARD"`:
  - NO_SHARD → DDP (since PyTorch deprecated FSDP's NO_SHARD). Replicates params, one grad all-reduce.
  - Otherwise → FSDP with `use_orig_params=True`, bf16 mixed precision, `SHARD_GRAD_OP` (default).
- `optimizer_target`: optimizer targets the inner trainable submodule (`model.draft_model`), not the whole composite. This is the `FSDP(model, ...)` over composite but optimizer over draft.
- frozen target tables (lm_head, embed_tokens) for DFlash family are kept replicated via `ignored_modules` (because sharding frozen inference-only tables wastes memory).
- DFlash-family `_noSplit_modules` → transformer_auto_wrap_policy for per-block wrapping (overlaps all-gather/reduce-scatter with compute). EAGLE models have single root FSDP unit.
- Optimizer is built AFTER wrapping, over the inner draft submodule. The `optimizer_state_is_replicated` is true for DDP.

Key point: `prepare_model` must wrap BEFORE strategy runs forward — the strategy must run forward through the wrapped module so FSDP is in the forward/backward path (not bypassed at >1 rank). This is in trainer.py L421-425 comment.

### backward/step

- `backward(loss, is_boundary)`: 
  - boundary or unwrapped → `loss.backward()`
  - non-boundary wrapped → `with self.module.no_sync(): loss.backward()`
- `step()`: just `self.optimizer.step()` which returns grad_norm.
- The optimizer (BF16Optimizer) owns the grad norm computation: `_grad_norm_and_clip_coefficient` computes total_norm_sq from the bf16 params, all-reduces across the FSDP process group via `_reduce_grad_norm`, then clip coefficient.
- `configure_grad_norm_reduction` is called by backend to wire the process group and disable reduction for NO_SHARD/DDP (replicated params).

### no_sync and accumulation boundary

- TrainerCore.train_step:
  - loss = out.loss / accumulation_steps (scale down so the SUM of N micro-batches equals one logical step)
  - _micro += 1
  - stepped = _micro % accumulation_steps == 0
  - backend.backward(loss, is_boundary=stepped)
  - grad_norm = backend.step() if stepped else None
  - returns optimizer_stepped=stepped
- The boundary is known BEFORE backward so the backend can defer the FSDP gradient reduction (no_sync) on non-boundary micro-steps.
- no_sync keeps gradients local (no reduce-scatter / all-reduce) until the boundary backward, where one reduction happens on the accumulated sum.
- If final backward is inside no_sync (remainder != 0), `_fit` raises RuntimeError — no partial step, no false checkpoint success.
- USP: accumulation_steps is multiplied by sp_size in assembly (L519-524) so one accumulation unit = one complete logical sequence (across sp ranks).

Why no_sync for non-boundary? Because gradient accumulation means we want to SUM gradients over N micro-batches before applying one optimizer step. If we reduced/synced gradients every micro-batch, we'd still need to sum them, but more importantly FSDP's reduce-scatter on every backward would be wasted communication. With no_sync, only the boundary backward triggers the collective, reducing communication by N×. Also, FSDP's `no_sync` keeps the full gradient accumulation working: gradients accumulate in the `.grad` buffers across the no_sync windows, and only the last backward reduces them.

Actually, let me be precise about what no_sync does in FSDP. In FSDP, `no_sync()` disables the gradient sync (reduce-scatter in SHARD_GRAD_OP) for that backward pass; gradients accumulate locally on each rank's sharded params. On the boundary backward (without no_sync), FSDP performs the reduce-scatter, summing the accumulated gradients across ranks. This is exactly the standard FSDP grad accumulation pattern.

For DDP, no_sync() similarly disables the gradient all-reduce; gradients accumulate, and the boundary backward triggers one all-reduce.

Why scale loss by 1/accumulation_steps? Because loss.backward() ADDS to .grad. If we do N backward passes each adding the full gradient, the accumulated grad would be N× the average. Dividing each loss by N makes the sum equal to the average gradient — equivalent to one large batch of N× samples. This is the standard gradient accumulation math.

Gradient accumulation: suppose accumulation_steps = N. Each micro-batch i contributes gradient g_i = ∂L_i/∂θ. Standard step wants the average over N batches: (1/N) Σ g_i. By scaling loss_i' = L_i/N, backward gives (1/N)g_i. Summing N of them via accumulation: Σ (1/N)g_i = (1/N)Σg_i. ✓
